stem: A pupil uses an Integrated Development Environment (IDE) to write and test a program.
model_answer: An IDE brings several programming tools together in one application. Typical features include: a code editor with syntax highlighting and auto-indent; a run/interpret button or build-and-run tool that translates the code; a debugger with breakpoints, step-through and variable watches to find logic errors; and error messages/explanations when code will not compile. These features make it quicker to write, test and fix programs.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Describe three features of an IDE and how each helps when writing a program.

mark_points.text: editor with syntax highlighting / auto-indent / auto-complete - makes code easier to read and type correctly
built-in translator / run button - compiles or interprets the code so the program can be tested
accepted_alternatives: run/build command lets you execute the program from the IDE

mark_points.text: debugger with breakpoints/step-through/variable watches - helps find and fix logic errors
accepted_alternatives: debugger lets you pause and inspect variables
